lede: A look at October 8 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ángela Aguilar and Kristanna Loken, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Second Nagorno-Karabakh War
events.desc: Second Nagorno-Karabakh War: Azerbaijan twice deliberately targeted the Church of the Holy Savior Ghazanchetsots of Shusha.

events.title: United States Department of Homeland Security
events.desc: U.S. president George W. Bush announces the establishment of the Office of Homeland Security.

events.title: Yom Kippur War
events.desc: Yom Kippur War: Israel loses more than 150 tanks in a failed attack on Egyptian-occupied positions.

events.title: Harrow and Wealdstone rail crash
events.desc: The Harrow and Wealdstone rail crash in England kills 112 people.

events.title: Battle of Crucifix Hill
events.desc: World War II: Captain Bobbie Brown earns a Medal of Honor for his actions during the Battle of Crucifix Hill, just outside Aachen.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Corporal Alvin C. York kills 28 German soldiers and captures 132, for which he was awarded the Medal of Honor.

events.title: War of the Pacific
events.desc: War of the Pacific: The Chilean Navy defeats the Peruvian Navy in the Battle of Angamos.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: The Confederate invasion of Kentucky is halted at the Battle of Perryville.

events.title: Siege of Alkmaar
events.desc: The Spanish siege of Alkmaar ends with the first Dutch victory in the Eighty Years' War.

events.title: Council of Chalcedon
events.desc: The first session of the Council of Chalcedon begins.

births.name: Betty Boothroyd
births.line: English politician, British Speaker of the House of Commons (died 2023)

deaths.text: Miguel Ángel Russo, Argentine footballer and manager
Mark Porter (racing driver), New Zealand race car driver
Ahmet Tevfik Pasha, Ottoman politician, 292nd Grand Vizier of the Ottoman Empire
Jean de Quen, French missionary, priest, and historian

observances.text: Christian feast day: Pelagia (Eastern Orthodox and Roman Catholic Churches)
Christian feast day: San Ernesto, Che Guevara as a folk saint. (Bolivian campesinos)
Christian feast day: William Dwight Porter Bliss and Richard T. Ely (Episcopal Church)
